feedback.text: If we buy something anywhere, we as consumers are the owners of the product you bought. Situations in which our rights of use for a given product are withdrawn because such a company responsible for the product in question had a captain. Be it physical or digital – if we have purchased a product, we are the owners of the product and we decide how we will use it. In the digital sphere, we would particularly need regulation to ensure that once we buy a product, we cannot collect it and that once we buy it, we will have the right to modify it for your own needs. However, Google wants to deny us the possibility of installing apps from sources other than their own shop (link https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2025/08/elevating-android-security.html). When buying electronic devices, we have the right to see both hardware and software. Removal of the right to view the interior of the device and to remove the possibility to modify it is an interference with our device choices. There is also a need to look at because more and more companies allow games to be online only and in multi-player mode, which is a source of concern. Players do not want to use a connection to launch the game, nor do they want to play each other without the internet or other players. Creators and publishers need to be forced to play games without connection to the network. It should also be done so that creators and publishers of these games give access to their production even after the aid expires. Payment intermediaries who, under the pretext, prohibit the purchase of a specific product group through their intermediary parties should also be held liable (example: Visa and Mastercard - https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/jul/29/mastercard-visa-backlash-adult-games-removed-online-stores-steam-itchio-ntwnfb). The idea of age verification to access any page we visit is a complete destruction of our privacy. Especially in the era when some platforms experience data leaks (recently also sensitive ones), the example of Discorda: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8jmzd972leo). What should be hoped for the information to be displayed on the site and that the user agrees to view the content on the site by entering it. Providing their sensitive personal data to anyone in the digital sphere will sooner or later lead to data leaks and anyone starts fearing the potential use of such data for criminal purposes. TL;DR: allow the continued use of the product after the end of the support, force offline mode in games, do not introduce age verification in any form, guarantee access to the “bebech” of the device, ensure that the product you buy can be modified, make payment intermediaries not restricting access to digital services/products
